title: is_private_host does not block decimal/octal IP representations or 0.0.0.0
description:
src/server.rs is_private_host() parses the host string as IpAddr. This correctly handles standard dotted-quad IPv4 and IPv6 addresses. However:

1. host.parse::<IpAddr>() in Rust's std parses only canonical forms. Non-canonical forms like decimal integers (http://2130706433/ = 127.0.0.1), octal (0177.0.0.1), or hex (0x7f000001) are NOT parsed by std and fall through to the hostname branch, which only checks 'localhost', '.local', '.internal'. These alternate forms are accepted by some HTTP clients.

2. reqwest (via hyper/hickory-dns) normalizes URLs through the url crate before connecting. The url crate does parse decimal integer IPs (e.g. url::Url::parse('http://2130706433/') resolves host_str() to '127.0.0.1'). So is_private_host('2130706433') would check if '2130706433' == 'localhost' → false → not blocked. But url::Url::parse would resolve it to 127.0.0.1.

3. The gap: is_private_host receives the raw host_str() from url::Url which DOES normalize to dotted-quad, so decimal IPs are actually safe via url crate. However a host of '0' (decimal zero) url::Url parses as host_str() = '0.0.0.0' which IS caught. This needs verification against actual reqwest/hyper behavior to confirm no gap exists.

Priority P2: theoretical, depends on url crate normalization behavior remaining stable.
close_reason: All pentest fixes: require Signature header, verify Digest, enforce actor-keyId match, Date freshness, body size limit 256KB, webhook key via header, decompression bomb limits, symlink rejection, redirect following disabled, delivery SSRF recheck, backslash escape handling
